title: silene colorata subsp. decumbens
intro: Silene colorata subsp. decumbens, also known as Decumbent Catchfly, is a plant species belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its pink to white, fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Silene colorata subsp. decumbens include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to relieve congestion, reduce inflammation, and promote overall respiratory health.
